004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.http;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
021
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.function.*;
024
025import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
028
029/**
030 * Describes a single media type used in content negotiation between an HTTP client and server, as described in
031 * Section 14.1 and 14.7 of RFC2616 (the HTTP/1.1 specification).
032 *
033 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
034 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauRestCommon">juneau-rest-common Basics</a>
035 *    <li class='extlink'><a class="doclink" href="https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc2616/rfc2616.html">Hypertext Transfer Protocol -- HTTP/1.1</a>
036 * </ul>
037 */
038@BeanIgnore
039@SuppressWarnings({
040   "java:S1700", // Field 'mediaType' intentionally shares the class name; it holds the canonical string form of this MediaType instance
041   "java:S1845"  // Field name intentionally matches method name
042})
043public class MediaType implements Comparable<MediaType> {
044
045   /** Represents an empty media type object. */
046   public static final MediaType EMPTY = new MediaType("/*");
047
048   private static final Cache<String,MediaType> CACHE = Cache.of(String.class, MediaType.class).build();
049
050   /** Reusable predefined media type */
051   public static final MediaType CSV = of("text/csv");
052   /** Reusable predefined media type */
053   public static final MediaType HTML = of("text/html");
054   /** Reusable predefined media type */
055   public static final MediaType JSON = of("application/json");
056   /** Reusable predefined media type for MessagePack binary format */
057   public static final MediaType MSGPACK = of("octal/msgpack");
058   /** Reusable predefined media type for plain text */
059   public static final MediaType PLAIN = of("text/plain");
060   /** Reusable predefined media type for UON (URL-Encoding Object Notation) */
061   public static final MediaType UON = of("text/uon");
062   /** Reusable predefined media type for URL-encoded form data */
063   public static final MediaType URLENCODING = of("application/x-www-form-urlencoded");
064   /** Reusable predefined media type for XML */
065   public static final MediaType XML = of("text/xml");
066   /** Reusable predefined media type for YAML */
067   public static final MediaType YAML = of("application/yaml");
068   /** Reusable predefined media type for SOAP XML */
069   public static final MediaType XMLSOAP = of("text/xml+soap");
070   /** Reusable predefined media type for RDF XML */
071   public static final MediaType RDF = of("text/xml+rdf");
072   /** Reusable predefined media type for RDF XML with abbreviations */
073   public static final MediaType RDFABBREV = of("text/xml+rdf+abbrev");
074   /** Reusable predefined media type for N-Triple RDF format */
075   public static final MediaType NTRIPLE = of("text/n-triple");
076   /** Reusable predefined media type for Turtle RDF format */
077   public static final MediaType TURTLE = of("text/turtle");
078   /** Reusable predefined media type for N3 (Notation3) RDF format */
079   public static final MediaType N3 = of("text/n3");
080   // @formatter:on
081
082   /**
083    * Returns the media type for the specified string.
084    * The same media type strings always return the same objects so that these objects
085    * can be compared for equality using '=='.
086    *
087    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
088    *    <li class='note'>
089    *       Spaces are replaced with <js>'+'</js> characters.
090    *       This gets around the issue where passing media type strings with <js>'+'</js> as HTTP GET parameters
091    *       get replaced with spaces by your browser.  Since spaces aren't supported by the spec, this
092    *       is doesn't break anything.
093    *    <li class='note'>
094    *       Anything including and following the <js>';'</js> character is ignored (e.g. <js>";charset=X"</js>).
095    * </ul>
096    *
097    * @param value
098    *    The media type string.
099    *    Will be lowercased.
100    *    Returns <jk>null</jk> if input is null or empty.
101    * @return A cached media type object.
102    */
103   public static MediaType of(String value) {
104      return value == null ? null : CACHE.get(value, () -> new MediaType(value));
105   }
106
107   /**
108    * Same as {@link #of(String)} but allows you to specify the parameters.
109    *
110    *
111    * @param value
112    *    The media type string.
113    *    Will be lowercased.
114    *    Returns <jk>null</jk> if input is null or empty.
115    * @param parameters The media type parameters.  If <jk>null</jk>, they're pulled from the media type string.
116    * @return A new media type object, cached if parameters were not specified.
117    */
118   public static MediaType of(String value, NameValuePair...parameters) {
119      if (parameters.length == 0)
120         return of(value);
121      return isEmpty(value) ? null : new MediaType(value, parameters);
122   }
123
124   /**
125    * Same as {@link #of(String)} but allows you to construct an array of <c>MediaTypes</c> from an
126    * array of strings.
127    *
128    * @param values
129    *    The media type strings.
130    * @return
131    *    An array of <c>MediaType</c> objects.
132    *    <br>Always the same length as the input string array.
133    */
134   public static MediaType[] ofAll(String...values) {
135      var mt = new MediaType[values.length];
136      for (var i = 0; i < values.length; i++)
137         mt[i] = of(values[i]);
138      return mt;
139   }
140
141   private static HeaderElement parse(String value) {
142      HeaderElement[] elements = HeaderValueParser.parseElements(emptyIfNull(trim(value)));
143      return (elements.length > 0 ? elements[0] : new HeaderElement("", NameValuePair.EMPTY_ARRAY));
144   }
145
146   private final String string;                          // The entire unparsed value.
147   private final String mediaType;                      // The "type/subtype" portion of the media type..
148   private final String type;                           // The media type (e.g. "text" for Accept, "utf-8" for Accept-Charset)
149   private final String subType;                        // The media sub-type (e.g. "json" for Accept, not used for Accept-Charset)
150   private final String[] subTypes;                     // The media sub-type (e.g. "json" for Accept, not used for Accept-Charset)
151   private final String[] subTypesSorted;               // Same as subTypes, but sorted so that it can be used for comparison.
152
153   private final boolean hasSubtypeMeta;                // The media subtype contains meta-character '*'.
154
155   private final NameValuePair[] parameters;            // The media type parameters (e.g. "text/html;level=1").  Does not include q!
156
157   /**
158    * Constructor.
159    *
160    * @param e The parsed media type string.
161    */
162   public MediaType(HeaderElement e) {
163      this(e, null);
164   }
165
166   /**
167    * Constructor.
168    *
169    * @param e The parsed media type string.
170    * @param parameters Optional parameters.
171    */
172   public MediaType(HeaderElement e, NameValuePair[] parameters) {
173      mediaType = e.getName();
174
175      if (parameters == null) {
176         parameters = e.getParameters();
177         for (var i = 0; i < parameters.length; i++) {
178            if (parameters[i].getName().equals("q")) {
179               parameters = Arrays.copyOfRange(parameters, 0, i);
180               break;
181            }
182         }
183      }
184      for (var i = 0; i < parameters.length; i++)
185         parameters[i] = new BasicNameValuePair(parameters[i].getName(), parameters[i].getValue());
186      this.parameters = parameters;
187
188      var x = mediaType.replace(' ', '+');
189      var i = x.indexOf('/');
190      type = (i == -1 ? x : x.substring(0, i));
191      subType = (i == -1 ? "*" : x.substring(i + 1));
192
193      subTypes = splita(subType, '+');
194      subTypesSorted = Arrays.copyOf(subTypes, subTypes.length);
195      Arrays.sort(this.subTypesSorted);
196      hasSubtypeMeta = CollectionUtils.contains("*", this.subTypes);
197
198      var sb = new StringBuilder();
199      sb.append(mediaType);
200      for (var p : parameters)
201         sb.append(';').append(p.getName()).append('=').append(p.getValue());
202      this.string = sb.toString();
203   }
204
205   /**
206    * Constructor.
207    *
208    * @param mt The media type string.
209    */
210   public MediaType(String mt) {
211      this(parse(mt));
212   }
213
214   /**
215    * Constructor.
216    *
217    * @param mt The media type string.
218    * @param parameters The media type parameters.  If <jk>null</jk>, they're pulled from the media type string.
219    */
220   public MediaType(String mt, NameValuePair[] parameters) {
221      this(parse(mt), parameters);
222   }
223
224   @Override
225   public final int compareTo(MediaType o) {
226      return toString().compareTo(o.toString());
227   }
228
229   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
230   public boolean equals(Object o) {
231      return (o instanceof MediaType o2) && eq(this, o2, (x, y) -> eq(x.string, y.string));
232   }
233
234   /**
235    * Performs an action on the additional parameters on this media type.
236    *
237    * @param action The action to perform.
238    * @return This object.
239    */
240   public MediaType forEachParameter(Consumer<NameValuePair> action) {
241      for (var p : parameters)
242         action.accept(p);
243      return this;
244   }
245
246   /**
247    * Performs an action on the subtypes broken down by fragments delimited by <js>"'"</js>.
248    *
249    * @param action The action to perform.
250    * @return This object.
251    */
252   public final MediaType forEachSubType(Consumer<String> action) {
253      for (var s : subTypes)
254         action.accept(s);
255      return this;
256   }
257
258   /**
259    * Returns the additional parameter on this media type.
260    *
261    * @param name The additional parameter name.
262    * @return The parameter value, or <jk>null</jk> if not found.
263    */
264   public String getParameter(String name) {
265      for (var p : parameters)
266         if (eq(name, p.getName()))
267            return p.getValue();
268      return null;
269   }
270
271   /**
272    * Returns the additional parameters on this media type.
273    *
274    * <p>
275    * For example, given the media type string <js>"text/html;level=1"</js>, will return a map
276    * with the single entry <code>{level:[<js>'1'</js>]}</code>.
277    *
278    * @return The map of additional parameters, or an empty map if there are no parameters.
279    */
280   public List<NameValuePair> getParameters() { return l(parameters); }
281
282   /**
283    * Returns the <js>'subType'</js> fragment of the <js>'type/subType'</js> string.
284    *
285    * @return The media subtype.
286    */
287   public final String getSubType() { return subType; }
288
289   /**
290    * Returns the subtypes broken down by fragments delimited by <js>"'"</js>.
291    *
292    * <P>
293    * For example, the media type <js>"text/foo+bar"</js> will return a list of
294    * <code>[<js>'foo'</js>,<js>'bar'</js>]</code>
295    *
296    * @return An unmodifiable list of subtype fragments.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
297    */
298   public final List<String> getSubTypes() { return l(subTypes); }
299
300   /**
301    * Returns the <js>'type'</js> fragment of the <js>'type/subType'</js> string.
302    *
303    * @return The media type.
304    */
305   public final String getType() { return type; }
306
307   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
308   public int hashCode() {
309      return string.hashCode();
310   }
311
312   /**
313    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the subtype contains the specified <js>'+'</js> delimited subtype value.
314    *
315    * @param st
316    *    The subtype string.
317    *    Case is ignored.
318    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the subtype contains the specified subtype string.
319    */
320   public final boolean hasSubType(String st) {
321      if (nn(st))
322         for (var s : subTypes)
323            if (eqic(st, s))
324               return true;
325      return false;
326   }
327
328   /**
329    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this media type subtype contains the <js>'*'</js> meta character.
330    *
331    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this media type subtype contains the <js>'*'</js> meta character.
332    */
333   public final boolean isMetaSubtype() { return hasSubtypeMeta; }
334
335   /**
336    * Given a list of media types, returns the best match for this <c>Content-Type</c> header.
337    *
338    * <p>
339    * Note that fuzzy matching is allowed on the media types where the <c>Content-Types</c> header may
340    * contain additional subtype parts.
341    * <br>For example, given a <c>Content-Type</c> value of <js>"text/json+activity"</js>,
342    * the media type <js>"text/json"</js> will match if <js>"text/json+activity"</js> or <js>"text/activity+json"</js>
343    * isn't found.
344    * <br>The purpose for this is to allow parsers to match when artifacts such as <c>id</c> properties are
345    * present in the header.
346    *
347    * @param mediaTypes The media types to match against.
348    * @return The index into the array of the best match, or <c>-1</c> if no suitable matches could be found.
349    */
350   public int match(List<MediaType> mediaTypes) {
351      int matchQuant = 0;
352      int matchIndex = -1;
353
354      for (var i = 0; i < mediaTypes.size(); i++) {
355         var mt = mediaTypes.get(i);
356         var matchQuant2 = mt.match(this, true);
357         if (matchQuant2 > matchQuant) {
358            matchQuant = matchQuant2;
359            matchIndex = i;
360         }
361      }
362      return matchIndex;
363   }
364
365   /**
366    * Returns a match metric against the specified media type where a larger number represents a better match.
367    *
368    * <p>
369    * This media type can contain <js>'*'</js> metacharacters.
370    * <br>The comparison media type must not.
371    *
372    * <ul>
373    *    <li>Exact matches (e.g. <js>"text/json"</js>/</js>"text/json"</js>) should match
374    *       better than meta-character matches (e.g. <js>"text/*"</js>/</js>"text/json"</js>)
375    *    <li>The comparison media type can have additional subtype tokens (e.g. <js>"text/json+foo"</js>)
376    *       that will not prevent a match if the <c>allowExtraSubTypes</c> flag is set.
377    *       The reverse is not true, e.g. the comparison media type must contain all subtype tokens found in the
378    *       comparing media type.
379    *       <ul>
380    *          <li>We want the <c>JsonSerializer</c> (<js>"text/json"</js>) class to be able to handle requests for <js>"text/json+foo"</js>.
381    *          <li>We want to make sure <c>Json5Serializer</c> (<js>"text/json5"</js>) does not handle
382    *             requests for <js>"text/json"</js>.
383    *       </ul>
384    *       More token matches should result in a higher match number.
385    * </ul>
386    *
387    * The formula is as follows for <c>type/subTypes</c>:
388    * <ul>
389    *    <li>An exact match is <c>100,000</c>.
390    *    <li>Add the following for type (assuming subtype match is &lt;0):
391    *    <ul>
392    *       <li><c>10,000</c> for an exact match (e.g. <js>"text"</js>==<js>"text"</js>).
393    *       <li><c>5,000</c> for a meta match (e.g. <js>"*"</js>==<js>"text"</js>).
394    *    </ul>
395    *    <li>Add the following for subtype (assuming type match is &lt;0):
396    *    <ul>
397    *       <li><c>7,500</c> for an exact match (e.g. <js>"json+foo"</js>==<js>"json+foo"</js> or <js>"json+foo"</js>==<js>"foo+json"</js>)
398    *       <li><c>100</c> for every subtype entry match (e.g. <js>"json"</js>/<js>"json+foo"</js>)
399    *    </ul>
400    * </ul>
401    *
402    * @param o The media type to compare with.
403    * @param allowExtraSubTypes If <jk>true</jk>,
404    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the media types match.
405    */
406   @SuppressWarnings({
407      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for media type matching logic
408   })
409   public final int match(MediaType o, boolean allowExtraSubTypes) {
410
411      if (o == null)
412         return -1;
413
414      // Perfect match
415      if (this == o || (type.equals(o.type) && subType.equals(o.subType)))
416         return 100000;
417
418      var c = 0;
419
420      if (type.equals(o.type))
421         c += 10000;
422      else if ("*".equals(type) || "*".equals(o.type))
423         c += 5000;
424
425      if (c == 0)
426         return 0;
427
428      // Subtypes match but are ordered different
429      if (eq(subTypesSorted, o.subTypesSorted))
430         return c + 7500;
431
432      for (var st1 : subTypes) {
433         if ("*".equals(st1))
434            c += 0;
435         else if (CollectionUtils.contains(st1, o.subTypes))
436            c += 100;
437         else if (o.hasSubtypeMeta)
438            c += 0;
439         else
440            return 0;
441      }
442      for (var st2 : o.subTypes) {
443         if ("*".equals(st2))
444            c += 0;
445         else if (CollectionUtils.contains(st2, subTypes))
446            c += 100;
447         else if (hasSubtypeMeta)
448            c += 0;
449         else if (! allowExtraSubTypes)
450            return 0;
451         else
452            c += 10;
453      }
454
455      return c;
456   }
457
458   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
459   public String toString() {
460      return string;
461   }
462}
